intro.title: The Elephant Abul-Abbas
intro.body: Charlemagne sent Isaac the Jew east as part of an embassy to Harun al-Rashid in 797. Isaac returned in 802 with caliphal gifts — silks, brass clocks, and Abul-Abbas the elephant — who walked from Baghdad to Aachen via Tunisia. Einhard's Vita Karoli records the elephant's death in 810.

stops.name: Baghdad
stops.note: Round City of Mansur, Abbasid capital. Isaac receives Harun's gifts in autumn 801.
stops.imagePrompt: Hand-coloured plate. Concentric round city plan, palace at center, elephant being led out toward the western gate.

stops.name: Damascus
stops.note: Caravan stage on the route to the Mediterranean. The Umayyad mosque is then forty years old.
stops.imagePrompt: Hand-coloured plate. Umayyad mosque courtyard with elephant resting in the shade, traders with caged falcons.

stops.name: Carthage / Tunis
stops.note: African coast embarkation point. Isaac winters here 801-802 waiting for safe sailing weather.
stops.imagePrompt: Hand-coloured plate. North African port in winter rain, elephant under a canopy, dhows being rerigged for the crossing.

stops.name: Portus Veneris
stops.note: Ligurian landing-point, October 801. The elephant cannot cross the Alps before winter and is housed at Vercelli.
stops.imagePrompt: Hand-coloured plate. Italian coastal harbour with steep hills, elephant being walked off a dhow on planks.

stops.name: Aachen
stops.note: Abul-Abbas reaches Charlemagne's palace on 20 July 802 (Royal Frankish Annals). He dies in 810 at the Rhine.
stops.imagePrompt: Hand-coloured plate. Palatine chapel of Aachen with elephant being led through the gate, Frankish nobles staring.
